skill.name: python-testing-patterns
skill.description: Guides Python developers in building reliable pytest suites with fixtures, mocks, parametrization, async tests, coverage, and CI patterns.
skill.summary: Build reliable Python test suites with pytest patterns, fixtures, mocks, coverage, and CI guidance.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment, and heuristic critical findings, but manual review found a documentation-only pytest guide. The cited patterns are fenced code examples, mocked API tests, fake local database URLs, placeholder keys, and public documentation links. No prompt injection, malicious intent, data exfiltration, or executable payload was found.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command findings point to Markdown code fences and pytest command examples inside SKILL.md. They are documentation snippets, not executable skill logic or hidden command invocation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are fenced Markdown blocks or comments showing how to run pytest. No script file, shell wrapper, or runtime command execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Mock Network Testing Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are sample Python tests that demonstrate mocking requests.get and requests.post against api.example.com. The examples do not transmit real data and are not executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API client is embedded in a documented test example and the tests patch request functions. The remaining URLs are public documentation links.

low_findings.title: Test Environment Variable Examples
low_findings.description: The environment findings are pytest monkeypatch examples using DATABASE_URL and api_key placeholders. They teach test isolation and do not collect or exfiltrate secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The values are local test URLs and fake keys inside examples. The only environment mutation uses pytest monkeypatch within a test case.

low_findings.title: Security Keyword Scanner Matches Without Dangerous Context
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and system reconnaissance flags match benign testing words such as test-driven, fixture documentation, model id fields, and database schema examples. No cryptographic primitive, reconnaissance command, or host probing logic appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found prose and ordinary pytest or SQLAlchemy examples at these locations. No evidence found of malicious cryptography, host enumeration, or prompt injection.

content.value_statement: Python teams often need consistent testing patterns across unit, integration, async, and CI workflows. This skill provides practical pytest examples for fixtures, mocking, parametrization, coverage, and maintainable test design.
actual_capabilities: Explains unit, integration, functional, and performance test patterns for Python projects.
Shows pytest fixtures for setup, teardown, scopes, parametrized fixtures, and shared conftest files.
Demonstrates mocking with unittest.mock, patch decorators, MagicMock, and mocked HTTP clients.
Covers async tests, property-based tests, database tests, temporary files, markers, and coverage reporting.
Provides CI examples for running pytest and coverage in GitHub Actions.
limitations: It is a written guide, not an automated test generator or test runner.
Examples use simplified applications and may need adaptation for production frameworks.
It focuses on pytest and common Python tooling, not every testing framework.
It does not inspect a repository or verify that generated tests pass.

use_cases.title: Add Tests to a Python Feature
use_cases.description: Create focused pytest cases for a new function, class, or service while keeping setup clear and repeatable.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Testing Patterns
use_cases.description: Document consistent approaches for fixtures, naming, markers, coverage, and CI test execution.

use_cases.title: Practice Advanced Pytest Techniques
use_cases.description: Learn how to test async code, database behavior, external APIs, temporary files, and generated inputs.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Pytest Tests
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for simple unit tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use python-testing-patterns to write pytest unit tests for this Python function. Include clear arrange, act, and assert sections.

prompt_templates.title: Design Fixture Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for reusable setup and teardown.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use python-testing-patterns to design pytest fixtures for this module. Include fixture scope choices and cleanup behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Mock External Services
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for isolated API tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use python-testing-patterns to test this API client without real network calls. Show the mock strategy and important assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Full Test Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for a project-wide testing plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use python-testing-patterns to create a testing strategy for this Python service. Cover unit, integration, async, database, coverage, and CI needs.

output_examples.input: A request to test a calculator module with normal and error cases.
output_examples.output: A concise pytest plan with separate tests for valid arithmetic, division by zero, invalid input, and parametrized edge cases.

output_examples.input: A request to test an HTTP client without calling the real service.
output_examples.output: A mock-based testing approach that patches request calls, checks response handling, and verifies expected call arguments.

output_examples.input: A request to improve a project test suite.
output_examples.output: A structured recommendation covering fixture reuse, clearer test names, markers, coverage thresholds, and CI execution.

best_practices: Keep each test focused on one behavior and name it after the expected result.
Use fixtures for repeated setup, and make cleanup explicit for shared resources.
Mock external services and environment state so tests are deterministic.
anti_patterns: Do not depend on test order or shared mutable state between tests.
Do not call real external APIs from unit tests when a mock will verify behavior.
Do not chase high coverage numbers without testing important edge cases.

faq.question: Does this skill run pytest for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples that an AI assistant can use when helping you write or improve tests.

faq.question: Is this only for unit tests?
faq.answer: No. It covers unit, integration, async, database, property-based, and CI testing patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with mocking API calls?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for patching HTTP calls and asserting mocked request behavior.

faq.question: Does it support pytest fixtures?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers fixture scopes, teardown with yield, autouse fixtures, shared conftest files, and parametrized fixtures.

faq.question: Can it guide coverage setup?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes pytest-cov commands and configuration examples for coverage reports and thresholds.

faq.question: Are the database and API values real secrets?
faq.answer: No. They are fake example values used to explain test patterns and environment isolation.
